what_it_does: This noticeboard item is a fork briefing that provides background and a mandate for building an operator interface and its underlying system. It tells the build-fork to research Tim's architecture first, take direction from Tim, and avoid using a lead-authored plan or design.
observations: Title is 'F0 · You are the fork — what this is'
Type is 'block' and source is 'claude_code' in the 'dragnet-development' channel
It links to parent item board://item-91983d4f via a 'part_of' edge
Created and updated timestamps are identical and it has a single 'filed' history entry
Body opens with an H1 'Fork briefing — build the operator interface + its system, PROPERLY (research-first)'
It explicitly states there is no prescribed solution, step-list, or design
It warns that the lead's knowledge of Tim's full architecture is partial
It frames the file as BACKGROUND + MANDATE, not a plan
standouts: Explicitly rejects a lead-authored plan to avoid bias
Mandates research-first learning of Tim's architecture directly from Tim
Frames the reader as 'the build-fork' and the file as background + mandate
concerns: The mandate is broad ('operator interface + its system, PROPERLY') and contains no measurable scope boundaries or acceptance criteria in this file
notes: This file is a research-first build mandate telling the fork to learn Tim's architecture from Tim and avoid lead-authored plans when building the operator interface.
questions: What is the exact scope of 'the operator interface + its system'?
Who or what is Tim, and where is the architecture documented?
What is the parent item board://item-91983d4f?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches content: it is background and mandate, not a plan.
intention_signals: It is trying to orient a build fork to independently research Tim's architecture and construct the operator interface and its system without being constrained by a lead's incomplete plan.
novelty: It explicitly forbids a lead-authored solution design in order to prevent architectural bias.
contribution: A clear, bias-resistant framing of a build mandate that separates background from plan and directs the builder to learn the existing architecture before designing the operator interface.
summary_for_embedding: Noticeboard item item-728e1e2b is a fork briefing in the dragnet-development channel that gives a research-first build mandate to construct the operator interface and its system by learning Tim's architecture directly from Tim, while rejecting lead-authored plans to avoid bias.
intention_for_embedding: To orient a build fork toward an unbiased, architecture-grounded implementation of the operator interface and its system.
